We recommended, and the management agreed that the LCE direct the:

(3) a. Inventory Committee/Property Custodian to:

1.Formulate plans and strategize the complete conduct of physical count of all its PPE, and prepare and submit the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of the ensuing year in compliance with Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I;

2.Reconcile their RPCPPE with the records of the Municipal Accountant; and

3.Ensure the report’s usability by including all the required information in the RPCPPE, as illustrated in the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II.

(4) b. Municipal Accountant to:

1.Recognize or reclassify the completed projects during the year into its proper PPE account; and

2.Provide depreciation for depreciable PPEs with an aggregate cost of ₱35,420,407.51 in accordance with Paragraphs 43 and 71 of IPSAS 17;

3.Recognize proper PPE accounts upon completion of infrastructure projects and maintain subsidiary ledgers to each project component; and

4. Submit the disbursement vouchers and 1 cancelled check specified in Table 2 to the Audit Team for evaluation.

(5) c. Create an Inventory Committee, who are temporarily relieved of all their regular duties, to complete the physical inventory in three (3) months or less, and to prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to the COA Audit Team at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities; and ensure full implementation of “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” as provided for in COA Circular 2020-006.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed that the LCE direct the:
Revenue Collectors to strictly require the remittance of collections intact to the Liquidating Officer, daily or the next working day;
Liquidating Officer to demand regular daily remittance of collections; and
Municipal Treasurer to deposit collections at least once a week; or where collections are minimal and daily deposit thereof becomes costly and impractical, to deposit it as soon as the collections reach ₱10,000.00.

We recommended, and the management agreed that the LCE  direct the:
Inventory Committee/Property Custodian to:
Formulate plans and strategize the complete conduct of physical count of all its PPE, and prepare and submit the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of the ensuing year in compliance with Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
Reconcile their RPCPPE with the records of the Municipal Accountant; and
Ensure the report’s usability by including all the required information in the RPCPPE, as illustrated in the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II.
Municipal Accountant to:
Recognize or reclassify the completed projects during the year into its proper PPE account; and
Provide depreciation for depreciable PPEs with an aggregate cost of ₱35,420,407.51 in accordance with Paragraphs 43 and 71 of IPSAS 17;
Recognize proper PPE accounts upon completion of infrastructure projects and maintain subsidiary ledgers to each project component; and
Submit the disbursement vouchers and 1 cancelled check specified in Table 2 to the Audit Team for evaluation.
Create an Inventory Committee, who are temporarily relieved of all their regular duties, to complete the physical inventory in three (3) months or less, and to prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to the COA Audit Team at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities; and ensure full implementation of “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” as provided for in COA Circular 2020-006.

We recommended, and the management agreed that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately open a separate account for their common Trust Fund to be used for those with specific purpose.
Moving forward, reflect the fund received to the pertinent Trust Fund account of the LGU, then submit a copy of the related deposit slip/bank memos to the Municipal Accountant for the preparation of appropriate adjusting entries in the books of accounts.

prepare and submit the FUR pertaining to the abovementioned financial assistance; and
make the necessary adjusting entry to correct the balance of the Due to LGUs account.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:
BAC Chairperson and Secretariat to submit the contract within five (5) working days from the time the contract is perfected;
Municipal Engineer to immediately submit justification on the unimplemented and delayed projects;
Bids and Awards Committee and the Municipal Engineer to expedite the procurement and implementation process to realize the programmed projects planned; and
Municipal Accountant to:
To reconcile with Budget Officer and recoup the overpayments made to Contractors and effect the proposed adjustment in Appendix G; and
To monitor the disbursement of every project to avoid over spending of certain fund.
This is without prejudice for the issuance of Notice of Disallowance, if warranted.

We recommended, and the management agreed that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to:
conduct a reconciliation of the prior years’ outstanding balance of ₱228,914.21; and
remit immediately the unremitted balance for CY 2023 of ₱1,407,446.37 along with the reconciled balance of Due to BIR account as of December 31, 2023.
Moving forward, to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 69 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and BIR Revenue Regulations No. 2-98, by remitting on time fund due to BIR to avoid the incurrence of interest, penalties and/or surcharges.

prepare the necessary adjusting entries to properly recognize/classify the sub accounts used pertaining to the withholding and remittances of GSIS’ Loans.
monitor and reconcile the amount withheld and the amount remitted for the current year.
Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to:
remit immediately the reconciled balance of Due to GSIS accounts as of December 31, 2023.

prepare the necessary adjusting entries and properly recognize/classify the sub accounts used pertaining to the withholding and remittances of Pag-Ibig Loans.
monitor and reconcile the amount withheld and the amount remitted for the current year.
Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to remit immediately the reconciled balance of Due to Pag-Ibig and Philhealth accounts as of December 31, 2023.

request and secure travel order from the Office of the Provincial Governor for all her local travels outside the province; and

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to exert effort in the verification of the reconciling items amounting
to P P34,069,167.35 in the General Fund and to effect the adjustments in the
books. Also, to prepare the bank reconciliation statements for the Special
Education Fund and Trust Fund and to submit the same together with the bank
statements, for audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to provide and maintain subsidiary records for each category of
PPE; to prepare adjusting entries for the recognition of the CY 2012 balances of
PPE with net book value of P5,530,244.66 based on acquisition cost; and to book
up the accumulated depreciation of the same. Likewise, provide depreciation
charges for these PPEs from CY 2013 to CY 2017.

Further, require the Municipal Engineer to provide report on the cost
and status of completed and on-going infrastructure projects to facilitate the
closing of the CIP accounts to the proper PPE accounts particularly for CY 2012
to CY 2016. Furthermore, require the Inventory Committee to submit the
RPCPPE to the Accountant for reconciliation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to require the settlement of the new cash advances by officers
and employees and to exert effort in determining the officials and employees
with outstanding cash advances in the previous years. The LCE thru the
Municipal Accountant should prepare demand letters for the liquidations of
the same.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant
and the BAC secretariat to submit for audit the disbursement vouchers and
supporting documents for the expenses made out of the 20% DF and purchase
orders and infrastructure contracts, respectively.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to
deposit the collections intact and promptly and cause the immediate deposit of
the balance of collection of the previous years and of the current year.

We recommended that Management:
a) Submit immediately for audit the disbursement vouchers and
supporting documents charged against the LDRRMF.
b) Require the LDRRMO to include in the LDRRMFIP the projects and
activities to be funded out of the unexpended balances of the
LDRRMF of the previous years;
c) Require the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain separate registries
for the LDRRMF-MOOE and Capital Outlay for the current year
and for the continuing appropriations of the previous years;
d) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Report on Sources
and Utilization of the LDRRMF using the form prescribed in Annex
A of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and submit the same to the Auditor
on or before the 15th day after the end of each month; and
e) Require the Municipal Accountant to determine the unexpended
balances of MOOE 70% as well as the 30% QRF of the previous years
and facilitate the transfer of the balances to the special trust fund.

We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) prepare and
approve an annual budget for the ensuing years as mandated under Sections 99
and 100(c) of RA 7160 and DepED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No.1,s. 2017.

Accountant to demand the submission of the Liquidation Report and supporting
documents for the cash advance granted.

We recommended that the LGU establish the LCPC and allocate at least 1% of the
IRA for the implementation of its programs, projects and activities as required in
Section 15 of Republic Act 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120
dated July 4, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and other officials
concerned be required to submit the above-mentioned reports for CY 2017 and
to comply with the submission of the same in the succeeding years to facilitate
the timey audit of the transactions and communication of the audit results and to
ensure good governance thru transparency and accountability.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be required to prepare
and submit the Comparison Statement of Budget and Actual Amounts for the

and record the transactions for CY 2013 to report reliable balances of accounts
in the financial statements.

submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements as required in Section 74 of
PD 1445 and Sections 3.2, 3.3 and 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-001 dated October
2, 1996.

liquidations of cash advances for payment of Emergency Shelter Allowance
(ESA) and for social pension for 1st quarter 2015 amounting to P8,700,000.00

nine (59) lacking disbursement vouchers amounting to P2,739,102.39 in the

financial reports in accordance with Section 347 of Republic Act 7160, Section

We recommend that Management: a) comply strictly with the provisions of
Republic Act 9184 and its IRR, b) submit immediately the related contracts
and supporting documents for review, c) discontinue the procurement of goods and
other items thru reimbursement basis, and d) submit the disbursement voucher
and supporting documents for the procurement of medicines, and lacking
supporting documents for the progress billing of infrastructure projects. This

and Disallowance in the post-audit of the transactions.

appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund as provided in
DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer be required
to prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF and
